stem_template: {name} is wearing a white T-shirt with the number '{N}' on it. {pronoun_subject} looks into a mirror. Which number does {pronoun_subject_lower} see in the mirror?

visual_manifest.art_brief: A child in a white T-shirt showing the number '{N}'. A vertical line represents a mirror next to them. On the mirror side, show the laterally inverted reflection of the number.
visual_manifest.alt_text: Mirror reflection problem with the number {N}.

misconceptions.feedback_child: A mirror flips things! Hold up your hand — which hand is the mirror you?
Good thinking about reversal! But mirrors flip left-right, not the digit order.
Let's think about what mirrors do. Try holding a finger up to a mirror!

socratic_feedback.on_correct: Brilliant! Mirrors flip things left-to-right. This is called reflection symmetry.
socratic_feedback.generic_incorrect: Hold up a finger and look at it in a mirror. Does it flip? Numbers flip too! Which one looks like a reflection?
socratic_feedback.encouragement_after_retry: Think about writing a number on paper and holding it up to a mirror. What happens?

author: AP
